elements.text: (ii) Other eligible sources of funds

elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower include:

elements.label: text

elements.text: of at least 3% of value (as described in Section 4203.1(a)) must come from Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal funds and/or other eligible sources of funds.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible Mortgage offering.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Sweat equity for Home Possible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sweat equity is credit for labor performed on the Mortgaged Premises and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: materials furnished for the Mortgaged Premises by the Borrower. This credit must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully explained and documented.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any labor performed must be completed in a skillful and workmanlike manner to
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the appraised value. A certification of completion (Form 442) must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained verifying the work has been completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Eligible repairs and improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sweat equity is an eligible source of funds in connection with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: repairs and improvements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repairs and improvements to be completed by the Borrower listed in the sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract and included in the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repairs or improvements reflected on the appraisal report that are outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time of the appraisal (no credit for work completed prior to the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: property inspection by the appraiser)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Determining the value of the sweat equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The value of the sweat equity that may be used as an eligible source of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: equals the value of the labor performed plus the value of the materials furnished,
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Labor: Value must be estimated by the appraiser or a cost estimating service,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and documented in the appraisal report or separately in the Mortgage file; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Materials: Value must be estimated by the appraiser or a cost estimating
elements.label: text

elements.text: service, or calculated using receipts from the purchase of materials. Estimates
elements.label: text

elements.text: or costs as evidenced by receipts must be documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Maximum LTV and TLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Home Possible Mortgages that use sweat equity as an eligible source of funds,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the maximum LTV/TLTV ratios in Section 4501.7(a)(i) apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)No cash out at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: If sweat equity is used as an eligible source of funds, the Borrower must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive cash back at closing. Excess funds must result in a reduction of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal balance on the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Sweat equity as a Down Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: When sweat equity is used as a source of funds for Down Payment, the full
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of the Borrower’s Down Payment can be in the form of sweat equity or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: combination of sweat equity and Borrower personal funds as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 4501.7(c)(i) and 4501.7(c)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Affordable Seconds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sweat equity can be combined with an Affordable Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (G)Special delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.14 for special delivery requirements for Home Possible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originated with sweat equity as a credit towards the Down Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or Closing Costs.

elements.text: Any labor performed must be completed in a skillful and workmanlike manner to
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the appraised value. A certification of completion (Form 442) must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained verifying the work has been completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Eligible repairs and improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sweat equity is an eligible source of funds in connection with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: repairs and improvements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repairs and improvements to be completed by the Borrower listed in the sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract and included in the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repairs or improvements reflected on the appraisal report that are outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time of the appraisal (no credit for work completed prior to the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: property inspection by the appraiser)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Determining the value of the sweat equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The value of the sweat equity that may be used as an eligible source of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: equals the value of the labor performed plus the value of the materials furnished,
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Labor: Value must be estimated by the appraiser or a cost estimating service,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and documented in the appraisal report or separately in the Mortgage file; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Materials: Value must be estimated by the appraiser or a cost estimating
elements.label: text

elements.text: service, or calculated using receipts from the purchase of materials. Estimates
elements.label: text

elements.text: or costs as evidenced by receipts must be documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Maximum LTV and TLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Home Possible Mortgages that use sweat equity as an eligible source of funds,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the maximum LTV/TLTV ratios in Section 4501.7(a)(i) apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)No cash out at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: If sweat equity is used as an eligible source of funds, the Borrower must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive cash back at closing. Excess funds must result in a reduction of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal balance on the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Sweat equity as a Down Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: When sweat equity is used as a source of funds for Down Payment, the full
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of the Borrower’s Down Payment can be in the form of sweat equity or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: combination of sweat equity and Borrower personal funds as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 4501.7(c)(i) and 4501.7(c)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Affordable Seconds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sweat equity can be combined with an Affordable Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (G)Special delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.14 for special delivery requirements for Home Possible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originated with sweat equity as a credit towards the Down Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or Closing Costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Proceeds from an Affordable Second or other secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the TLTV ratio exceeds 97%, the secondary financing must be an Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Funds provided by an Agency that is affiliated with, under contract to or financed
elements.label: text

elements.text: (directly or indirectly) by the Seller as the originating lender, when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ The source of funds is an eligible source meeting all applicable Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements (for example, a gift or grant from an Agency must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 5501.4);
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ A contribution of at least 3% of value (as described in Section 4203.1(a)) is made
elements.label: text

elements.text: from Borrower personal funds and/or other eligible sources of funds as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in this section; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ The source of funds is not funded through the Mortgage transaction (including
elements.label: text

elements.text: differential pricing in rate, discount points, or fees for individual loans or across
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Home Possible Mortgage offering)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Flexible sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Home Possible Mortgages, flexible sources of funds include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Financing concessions: As described in Section 5501.6(b) meeting the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: interested party contribution requirements of Section 5501.6
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Lender credit: As described in Section 5501.7 and as documented on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: The required coverage levels for mortgage insurance for Home Possible® Mortgages are stated in

elements.label: text

elements.text: 4501.9: Homeownership education and landlord
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: education for Home Possible® Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Homeownership education
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Landlord education (2- to 4-unit Primary Residences)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-purchase and Early Delinquency Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Homeownership education
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one occupying Borrower must participate in a homeownership education program
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Note Date, or the Effective Date of Permanent Financing for Construction to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages, in each of the following instances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For purchase transactions when all occupying Borrowers are First-Time Homebuyers, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For any transaction when the credit reputation for all Borrowers is established using only
elements.label: text

elements.text: Noncredit Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The following are acceptable homeownership education programs:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Programs provided by HUD-approved counseling agencies, housing finance agencies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: community development financial institutions or mortgage insurance companies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s free homeownership education course, CreditSmart® Homebuyer U
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Programs that meet the National Industry Standards for Homeownership Education and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5103.4 for other requirements related to homeownership education.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Landlord education (2- to 4-unit Primary Residences)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For purchase transactions, at least one qualifying Borrower must participate in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: landlord education program before the Note Date, or the Effective Date of Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Landlord education must not be provided by an interested party to the transaction, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: originating lender or the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A copy of a certificate evidencing successful completion of the landlord education
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For refinance transactions, landlord education is not required but is recommended for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers who have not previously attended a program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Post-purchase and Early Delinquency Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller, as Servicer, must provide (at no cost to the Borrower) Early Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counseling to all Borrowers who experience problems meeting their Mortgage obligations, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Sections 9101.2(e) and 9102.4(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Credit Fees related to Home Possible® Mortgages, Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fees, and Exhibit 19A, Credit Fee Cap Eligibility Criteria. Credit Fees are paid in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.1: Overview for Community Land Trust (10/01/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Under the Community Land Trust model, the Community Land Trust owns land and leases it
elements.label: text

elements.text: under a long-term ground lease to a homebuyer who purchases the structure on the land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Community Land Trust Mortgages that meet the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter and the Guide. The requirements include use of a specified form of ground lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“Community Land Trust Ground Lease”) and Form 490, Community Land Trust Ground Lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rider, each as further specified in Section 4502.7.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Community Land Trust that is a non-profit organization exempt from taxation under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code is considered an Agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.2: Eligible Borrowers for Community Land Trust
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on its focus on affordability, the Community Land Trust typically has Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in addition to restrictions on the resale of the property improvements. Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers must meet the program requirements established by the Community Land Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Completion, execution and recordation of the Community Land Trust Ground Lease serves as
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmation that the Borrower has met the eligibility requirements set forth by the Community
elements.label: text

elements.text: Land Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.4: Loan purpose, eligible property and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: occupancy types and underwriting requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgages (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains subsections related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible property and occupancy types
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Community Land Trust Mortgage may be a purchase, “no cash-out” refinance or cash-out
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements of Chapter 4301 relating to refinance Mortgages, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance transaction must comply with the applicable requirements of the Community Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust, including, but not limited to, the amount of the refinance Mortgage and, in the case of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a cash-out refinance Mortgage, the amount of proceeds disbursed to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain and retain in the Mortgage file documentation signed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust or its authorized representative permitting the Borrower to enter into
elements.label: text

elements.text: a refinance transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible property and occupancy types
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Community Land Trust Mortgage must be secured by a 1- or 2-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is not a Manufactured Home. A Community Land Trust Mortgage secured by a 1-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence may be a CHOICEHome®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For requirements for CHOICEHome Mortgages, see Section 5703.12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Community Land Trust Mortgage may be submitted to Loan Product Advisor® or may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Manually Underwritten Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Community Land Trust Mortgages, minimum Down Payment requirements are
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on the purchase price.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.5: Maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) TLTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HTLTV) ratios and value for Community Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages (05/07/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: HTLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Value
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: The LTV, TLTV and HTLTV ratios are based upon the appraised value of the leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, without the resale and other restrictions included in the Community Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ground Lease.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4203.1(b) for maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios for purchase and “no
elements.label: text

elements.text: cash-out” refinance Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratio for cash-out refinance Mortgages is 65%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Value
elements.label: text

elements.text: For both purchase and refinance transactions, “value” is the appraised value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises on the Note Date. The appraised value must be determined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 4502.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The LTV ratio is obtained by dividing the First Lien Mortgage amount by the appraised
elements.label: text

elements.text: value as illustrated below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: LTV ratio calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraised value $300,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase price $225,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subsidy amount $75,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: First Lien Mortgage amount $225,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: LTV ratio 75%
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum required Borrower contribution for Down Payment* $0
elements.label: text

elements.text: *Minimum required Borrower contribution for Down Payment is determined by the product
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected for Mortgages with an LTV of greater than 80%. See Section 5501.3(k).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The purchase price excludes the subsidy amount that was provided to acquire the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For this reason, the purchase price may not be a reliable indicator of appraised value since it
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflects the subsidy amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The LTV ratio is obtained by dividing the First Lien Mortgage amount by the “value” as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.6: Appraisal requirements for Community Land
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Trust Mortgages (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the other Guide requirements related to appraisals, an appraisal for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgage must meet the requirements in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires the appraiser to develop the opinion of value for the leasehold interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on the hypothetical condition that the property rights being appraised are the leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest without the resale and other restrictions included in the Community Land Trust Ground
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lease, which are removed by Form 490, Community Land Trust Ground Lease Rider, upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal requirements include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must ensure that the appraiser is knowledgeable and experienced in appraising a
elements.label: text

elements.text: property subject to a leasehold estate held by a Community Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraiser must analyze the property subject to the Community Land Trust Ground Lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraiser must describe the terms and restrictions of the Community Land Trust Ground
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lease and Form 490
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraised value of the property must be well supported and correctly developed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraiser must develop the opinion of value for the leasehold interest based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: hypothetical condition that the property rights being appraised are the leasehold interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: without the resale and other restrictions included in the Community Land Trust Ground
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lease, which are removed by Form 490 upon foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ On the appraisal report form, the appraiser must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Indicate “leasehold” as the property rights appraised
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Report the applicable ground rent paid to the Community Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a property subject to a leasehold interest created by a Community Land Trust, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser should use sales of similar properties that are subject to other types of leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests as comparable sales. If this is not possible, the appraiser may use sales of properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are owned in fee simple. The appraiser should make appropriate adjustments to reflect
elements.label: text

elements.text: the differences in market value for the properties subject to the other types of leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests based on the terms of leases and the properties that are owned in fee simple.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Market Area has sales activity for other leasehold interests created by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust, the appraiser should analyze and report them in the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not use them as comparable sales if their sales prices were impacted or limited by
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions in the ground lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Because Freddie Mac’s appraisal report forms do not include space to provide all the details
elements.label: text

elements.text: required for appraising a property subject to a leasehold interest held by a Community Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust, the appraiser must attach an addendum to the appraisal report to provide any
elements.label: text

elements.text: information that cannot otherwise be presented on the appraisal report form. The appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: must check the box “as is” and include in the addendum an expanded discussion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales used and considered. The addendum must also include the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: “This appraisal is made based on the hypothetical condition that the property rights being
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraised are the leasehold interest without resale and other restrictions that are removed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Form 490 upon foreclosure.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.7: Requirements for Community Land Trust
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Ground Leases and Ground Lease Riders (10/01/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains sub-sections related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Ground Lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estate eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Ground Lease Rider
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage file requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Community Land Trust Ground Lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Ground lease requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must review the Community Land Trust’s ground lease and determine whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: it is based on the National Community Land Trust Network (NCLTN) 2011 Community
elements.label: text

elements.text: Land Trust Network Model Ground Lease or the Institute for Community Economics
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ICE) Model Ground Lease (collectively, the “model ground leases”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Community Land Trust programs included in Fannie Mae’s Certified Shared Equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Program list, which is available at https://singlefamily.fanniemae.com/originating￾underwriting/mortgage-products/shared-equity-programs, the Seller may sell such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgages to Freddie Mac, provided that,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Community Land Trust’s ground lease is based on one of the two model ground
elements.label: text

elements.text: leases, the Seller is not required to comply with the additional ground lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 4502.7(a)(ii); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Community Land Trust’s ground lease is not based on either of the two model
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground leases, the Seller must confirm that the ground lease complies with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional ground lease requirements in Section 4502.7(a)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The resale restrictions in the Community Land Trust Ground Lease can be imposed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: State or local governments, municipalities, instrumentalities or nonprofit entities to create
elements.label: text

elements.text: and preserve affordable housing (including entities administering governmental￾sponsored subsidy programs).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Community Land Trust Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac, the Seller represents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants that the Community Land Trust Ground Lease is valid, enforceable and in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: force and effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Additional ground lease requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must confirm that the Community Land Trust Ground Lease:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has a term of at least 30 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Includes a resale formula that limits the homeowner’s proceeds at resale. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions must be binding on current and subsequent property owners and remain in
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect (i.e., survive) until they are formally removed, are modified or terminate
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically in accordance with their terms, such as at a foreclosure sale or upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provides the Community Land Trust or its assignee the right to a preemptive option to
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase the home from the homeowner at resale (“the right of first refusal”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requires the Community Land Trust to review and approve any refinances and
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing, including Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requires that the homeowner use the leased land and the improvements primarily for
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential uses
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac prior written approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Community Land Trust’s ground lease is not based on either of the two model
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground leases or is not included in Fannie Mae’s Certified Shared Equity Program list,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s prior written approval is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With prior written approval from Freddie Mac, Community Land Trusts certified under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-approved certification program may be exempt from Seller review of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust’s ground lease, and Sellers will be relieved from enforcement of
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations and warranties that the Community Land Trust Ground Lease is valid,
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforceable and in full force and effect. The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval before selling Mortgages without review of the Community Land Trust’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground lease by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or Customer Service at 800-
elements.label: text

elements.text: FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Excess proceeds for Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Community Land Trust may be entitled to any applicable excess proceeds when
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is a transfer of title on a Community Land Trust property that occurs as a result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: such property being sold by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower for an amount exceeding the resale restricted price, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac after acquiring title through a completed foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excess proceeds on Community Land Trust properties are those proceeds that are above
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amount required to satisfy the total indebtedness, including any additional liens,
elements.label: text

elements.text: claims or encumbrances, in addition to any amount(s) incurred during an REO holding
elements.label: text

elements.text: period if title was acquired by Freddie Mac via a completed foreclosure sale or deed-in￾lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon satisfaction of the total indebtedness as outlined above, any excess proceeds should
elements.label: text

elements.text: be distributed as outlined below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For properties that are sold by the Borrower for an amount exceeding the resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted price agreed upon in the Community Land Trust Ground Lease:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First to the Borrower and Community Land Trust for equity due as agreed upon in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Community Land Trust Ground Lease, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All remaining excess proceeds to the Community Land Trust when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Ground Lease includes terms for excess proceeds to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: distributed to the Community Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For properties that are sold by Freddie Mac after acquiring title through a completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First to Freddie Mac for any amount(s) incurred during an REO holding period, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All remaining excess proceeds to the Community Land Trust when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Ground Lease includes terms for excess proceeds to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: distributed to the Community Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Servicers will not be responsible for these REO activities. For more information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer responsibilities on REO properties, see Chapter 9601.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Leasehold estate eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The leasehold estate created by the Community Land Trust Ground Lease must constitute
elements.label: text

elements.text: real property under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Community Land Trust ground lease rider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has developed Form 490, Community Land Trust Ground Lease Rider, that
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be completed, executed and recorded in the land records, together with the Community
elements.label: text

elements.text: Land Trust Ground Lease.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae’s Form 2100, Community Land Trust Ground Lease Rider, may be used in lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Form 490. Requirements pertaining to Form 490 in the Guide apply to Fannie Mae’s Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2100, when applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Mortgage file requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must contain an original executed or certified copy of the Community
elements.label: text

elements.text: Land Trust Ground Lease and the Form 490 containing the recordation information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Community Land Trust program is included in Fannie Mae’s Certified Shared Equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Program list, the Seller must include in the Mortgage file evidence that the Community Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust program is included in Fannie Mae’s list as of the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.8: Security Instruments for Community Land
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Trust Mortgages (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Security Instrument must describe the Mortgaged Premises as a leasehold interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: created by a recorded ground lease in the property described in the legal description. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must comply with Exhibit 5A, Authorized Changes to Notes, Riders, Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments and the Uniform Residential Loan Application, Section IV, for Leasehold Estates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.9: Title insurance requirements for Community
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Land Trust Mortgages (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgages must be insured by a title insurance policy or a policy and
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements that meet the requirements of Chapter 4702. The title insurance policy or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement to the policy must ensure the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Community Land Trust Ground Lease or ground lease memorandum was recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 490, Community Land Trust Ground Lease Rider, was recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Community Land Trust Mortgage is a First Lien on the leasehold estate and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are no existing mortgages, loans or other liens on the fee estate, except as may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted under Form 490
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Community Land Trust Ground Lease reversionary interest is subordinate to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgage; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are no related Community Land Trust Ground Lease occupancy and resale restrictions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants or agreements that are valid, existing or effective as of the date of the policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: except as may be permitted under Form 490
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4502.11: Landlord education (03/05/25)

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by a 2-unit property, at least one qualifying Borrower must meet one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following requirements:

elements.text: 4503.1: Shared equity programs – income-based

elements.text: resale restrictions (12/04/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions are eligible for sale
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.label: section_header

elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s other Purchase Documents are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as HeritageOne®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.

elements.text: 4504.2: Overview of HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approved Sellers of HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Negotiated underwriting provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ First Lien priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assumable Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Approved Sellers of HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval to sell HeritageOne Mortgages to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or Customer Service at 800-
elements.label: text

elements.text: FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Negotiated underwriting provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless specifically made applicable to HeritageOne Mortgages, negotiated underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions in the Seller’s Purchase Documents do not apply to HeritageOne Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.text: cannot be used with HeritageOne Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: As used in this chapter, the following terms have the meanings ascribed to them below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms for HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: BIA U.S. Department of the Interior Bureau of Indian Affairs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: American Tribe
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Native American Tribe that either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is listed as a participating tribe in HUD’s most recent Section 184
elements.label: text

elements.text: Participating Tribes List; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has entered into a memorandum of understanding with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: that, to the Seller’s knowledge, is and will remain in full force and
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: A list of Eligible Native American Tribes is available on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: N
elements.label: text

elements.text: Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: American Tribe
elements.label: text

elements.text: A federally recognized Indian tribe of the United States that is included
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the BIA’s most recent publication of the notice titled “Indian Entities
elements.label: text

elements.text: Recognized by and Eligible To Receive Services From the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bureau of Indian Affairs”. “Native American Tribe” includes any
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing authority, utility, corporation, partnership or other entity that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: partially or wholly owned by the Native American Tribe.
elements.label: text

elements.text: T
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribal Area A census tract, or any portion thereof, that is designated as both a HUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: Indian Housing Block Grant (IHBG) Area and a rural area under
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHFA’s Duty to Serve Regulation and is identified as an “Indian area”
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the current version of the FHFA’s Indian Areas File.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Eligible property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises must be located in the Tribal Area of an Eligible Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribe.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages must be secured by 1- to 4-unit Primary Residences, which may include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured Homes, including Manufactured Homes that are CHOICEHomes®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Units located in Planned Unit Developments (PUDs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties with ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) First Lien priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must comply with all statutory and regulatory requirements and obtain all
elements.label: text

elements.text: approvals and signatures necessary to establish and maintain a valid First Lien on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Security Instrument must be signed by all individuals or entities with an ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in the Mortgaged Premises. See Section 4101.6 for additional information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: signatures required for the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If necessary to establish or maintain the First Lien, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain any necessary approvals or signatures from the U.S. Secretary of the Interior or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the BIA, as applicable; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Record the Security Instrument and all other Mortgage documents required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded in the appropriate jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even if not necessary to establish or maintain the First Lien, it is recommended that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument and all other Mortgage documents required to be recorded also be
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded in the locality in which the Mortgaged Premises is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Assumable Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold under the Guarantor program that meet the requirements of Section 4101.9(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and this chapter may be assumable Mortgages, if permitted under the Seller’s Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Special delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.50 for special delivery requirements for HeritageOne Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Special Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 8406.1(d) and Chapter 8901 for special Servicing requirements for HeritageOne
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, including requirements for post-closing Borrower counseling, Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing and Transfers of Ownership.

elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence

elements.text: (d) Ownership of other property
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Note Date or, for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, the Effective Date of Permanent Financing, occupying Borrowers must not have
elements.label: text

elements.text: an ownership interest in more than two financed residential properties, including the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.

elements.text: 4504.5: General eligibility requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: HeritageOne® Mortgages (04/12/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Homebuyer education
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Landlord education for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following general eligibility requirements apply to HeritageOne® Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan amount may not exceed the applicable maximum original loan amount specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4203.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be either a purchase transaction Mortgage or a “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Temporary subsidy buydown plans described in Section 4204.3 are permitted if the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a 1-unit property; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Accept Mortgage secured by a 2-unit property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are permitted secondary financing arrangements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secondary financing that meets the requirements of Section 4204.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Affordable Seconds® that meet the requirements of Section 4204.2, except that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For Affordable Seconds provided by an Agency, although Section 4204.2(a)(i)(A)
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides otherwise, the Agency may be affiliated with, under contract to or financed
elements.label: text

elements.text: (directly or indirectly) by the Seller as the originating lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For Seller-funded Affordable Seconds, although Section 4204.2(a)(i)(B) provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise, the First Lien Mortgage cannot be a Home Possible® Mortgage and may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: a HeritageOne Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes and originated with Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seconds, although Section 5703.5(c) provides otherwise, the Mortgage cannot be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible Mortgage and may be a HeritageOne Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage with a temporary subsidy buydown plan, any secondary financing must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: a fixed interest rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: When determining the stable monthly income (as described in Section 5301.1), rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be considered if generated from:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A subject 1-unit property, with or without an ADU, if the rental income requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4501.6(b) or 5306.1(h) are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An ADU on a subject 1-unit property, if the rental income requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5306.1(g) are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A subject 2- to 4-unit property, if the rental income requirements in Section 5306.1(e) are
elements.label: text

elements.text: met and excluding any rental income generated from an ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A non-subject investment property, if the rental income requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5306.1(c) are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Homebuyer education
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transaction Mortgages where all occupying Borrowers are First-Time
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homebuyers:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one occupying Borrower must complete a homeownership education program
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Note Date or, for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, the Effective Date of Permanent Financing; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As referenced in Section 5103.4(b), homeownership education may be provided by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Native community development financial institution (CDFI), even if the Native CDFI is
elements.label: text

elements.text: the originating lender and/or the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5103.4 for other requirements related to homeownership education.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Landlord education for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transaction Mortgages, at least one qualifying Borrower must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete a landlord education program before the Note Date or, for Construction to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages, the Effective Date of Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have at least one year of previous landlord experience
elements.label: text

elements.text: For “no cash-out” refinance Mortgages, landlord education is not required but is
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommended for Borrowers who have not previously completed a landlord education
elements.label: text

elements.text: program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Landlord education may not be provided by an interested party to the Mortgage transaction,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except for a Native CDFI. A Native CDFI may provide landlord education even if the Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: CDFI is the originating lender and/or the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises may be subject to resale restrictions if the requirements of Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406 are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any right of first refusal must run to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Eligible Native American Tribe within whose Tribal Area the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any lessor other than the Eligible Native American Tribe (if applicable)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Mortgage is subject to foreclosure proceedings and/or an approved short sale, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right of first refusal may not continue beyond 120 days after the date of written notice that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises is being offered for sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any right of first refusal may not adversely impact the rights of Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to foreclose or acquire title to the Mortgaged Premises, accept a deed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment in lieu of foreclosure or transfer title to or lease the Mortgaged Premises, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4504.8: HeritageOne® Mortgages that are leasehold
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (03/05/25)

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lease agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form of lease agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Leasehold Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For leasehold Mortgages, the Mortgaged Premises must be located on either tribal trust land
elements.label: text

elements.text: or allotted trust land and cannot be located on land that is owned in fee simple.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Leasehold Mortgages must meet the requirements of Chapter 5704 and all other provisions in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller’s Purchase Documents applicable to leasehold estates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If permitted under the Seller’s Purchase Documents, Leasehold Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes must meet the requirements of Chapter 5706 and all other provisions in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller’s Purchase Documents applicable to Manufactured Homes, except that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home is not required to be located in a ground lease community (although
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5706.2 provides otherwise).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Lease agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The term of the lease agreement must be a minimum of 25 years with an automatic 25-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: renewal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The term of the lease agreement (before renewal) must expire at least five years after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: term of the Mortgage expires. Any necessary approvals or signatures must be obtained from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the U.S. Secretary of the Interior or the BIA, as applicable, to extend the term of the lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement to a period longer than 25 years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must maintain in the Mortgage file a copy of the recorded lease agreement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including any amendments or riders thereto.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Form of lease agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: It is recommended (but not required) that the most recent version of the standard lease form
elements.label: text

elements.text: in HUD’s Section 184 Program be used for leasehold Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lease agreement may not adversely impact the rights of Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to foreclose or acquire title to the Mortgaged Premises, accept a deed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment in lieu of foreclosure or transfer title to or lease the Mortgaged Premises, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4504.9: Appraisal requirements for HeritageOne®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraiser qualifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal type, automated collateral evaluation (ACE) eligibility and ACE+ PDR eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraised value representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal cost offset credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales comparison approach and cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional form instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Site value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Collateral Data Portal® (UCDP®)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional resources
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following appraisal requirements apply to HeritageOne® Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Appraiser qualifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the appraiser qualifications in Section 5603.1, the Seller must ensure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser has the knowledge and experience required to perform appraisals for properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: located in Tribal Areas. By way of example and not of limitation, the appraiser must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledgeable about and have experience with the various types of land ownership interests
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., tribal trust land, allotted trust land, unrestricted or restricted fee simple land, etc.) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: have access to all appropriate data sources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although traditional appraisal data sources (e.g., multiple listing service, public land records,
elements.label: text

elements.text: etc.) remain important sources of data, the appraiser may need access to other sources of data
elements.label: text

elements.text: and information, such as those maintained by the BIA and the Eligible Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribe within whose Tribal Area the Mortgaged Premises is located, to develop a fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: supported and sufficiently documented opinion of market value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appraisal type, ACE eligibility and ACE+ PDR eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal must meet the requirements of Topic 5600.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless ACE or ACE+ PDR is offered and accepted in accordance with Section 5602.3 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5602.4, respectively:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An appraisal report with an interior and exterior inspection is required; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must maintain in the Mortgage file all property valuation documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Appraised value representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an appraisal report is obtained and the sales comparison approach was used, the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be eligible for appraised value representation and warranty relief, if the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Collateral Advisor® appraised value representation and warranty relief in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5602.2 are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Appraisal cost offset credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an appraisal report is obtained for the Mortgage transaction, Freddie Mac will issue an
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal cost offset credit, which must be passed to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal cost offset credit will be detailed in the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Sales comparison approach and cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a sufficient number of comparable sales (i.e., a minimum of three closed comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales) are available, the sales comparison approach (with any support provided from the cost

elements.text: Chapter 4601: Financed Permanent Buydown
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4601.1: Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgage is a Mortgage for which the Borrower has
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanently reduced the interest rate by financing discount points in the loan amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Calculation of applicable Credit Fees for Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be an eligible fixed-rate, level-payment Mortgage or a 5/6-Month, 7/6-
elements.label: text

elements.text: Month or 10/6-Month ARM.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages must not be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HomeOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with a temporary subsidy buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by property subject to income-based resale restrictions that terminate upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required foreclosure redemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, where the property value must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 4406.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this chapter, the following definitions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms for Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Base Mortgage Amount The Mortgage amount without the financed discount
elements.label: text

elements.text: points
elements.label: text

elements.text: G
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gross loan-to-value (LTV),
elements.label: text

elements.text: total LTV (TLTV) and Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Equity Line of Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: The LTV, TLTV or HTLTV ratio calculated using the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage amount, which includes the financed discount
elements.label: text

elements.text: points
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility of Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages is determined using the Gross LTV,
elements.label: text

elements.text: TLTV and HTLTV ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages must comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Gross LTV, TLTV and HTLTV ratios must not exceed the LTV, TLTV or HTLTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratios specified in Section 4203.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The amount of the mortgage insurance coverage must meet the coverage level
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 4701.1, using the Gross LTV ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The maximum amount a Borrower can finance for a permanent buydown is three
elements.label: text

elements.text: discount points, calculated based upon the Base Mortgage Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. For fixed-rate Mortgages, Borrower qualification is based on the monthly housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense-to-income ratio calculated using the monthly payment at the permanent bought
elements.label: text

elements.text: down Note Rate. For ARMs, Borrower qualification is based on monthly payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated in accordance with Section 4401.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. For ARMs, the permanent buydown is in effect for the initial Note Rate and each Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate adjustment for the entire term of the Mortgage. The Lifetime Ceiling will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated using the permanent bought down initial Note Rate. The permanent buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not affect the Margin, Initial Cap or Periodic Cap.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Calculation of applicable Credit Fees for Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Applicable Credit Fees will be assessed and billed based on the UPB of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including the financed permanent buydown points) and the Gross LTV ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fee provisions stated in Chapter 6303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview of One-Time Close transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a One-Time Close transaction, the Note and the Security Instrument documenting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permanent Financing are executed at the time of closing for the Interim Construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing. The terms of the Interim Construction Financing are incorporated into the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the Permanent Financing. A One-Time Close transaction must be converted to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing either through automatic conversion or a modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a One-Time Close transaction, the construction phase of the Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage must be structured as a temporary loan exempt from the ability-to-repay
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under Regulation Z.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) One-Time Close with automatic conversion documentation structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a One-Time Close with automatic conversion, Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically converts to Permanent Financing upon completion of construction or
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations with no change to the terms of the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a One-Time Close with automatic conversion, the Mortgage file must include all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i)Determining Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage may be a purchase transaction Mortgage or a refinance Mortgage as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is a purchase transaction Mortgage if, prior to the closing of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interim Construction Financing, the Borrower is not the owner of record of the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Mortgaged Premises, or is not the lessee of the leasehold estate, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is a refinance Mortgage if, prior to the closing of the Interim
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction Financing, the Borrower is the owner of record of the land or Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises, or is the lessee of the leasehold estate, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii)Requirements for refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)“No cash-out” refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages must meet the requirements in Section 4301.4,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except as stated below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of Section 4301.4, the amount of the Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by the Mortgaged Premises is considered an amount used to pay off the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage as described in Section 4301.4. The proceeds of the Permanent Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be used to pay off a junior lien(s) secured by the Mortgaged Premises provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lien(s) were used in their entirety for the construction or renovation of the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, as applicable, as documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Paying off unsecured lien(s) or construction costs paid by the Borrower outside of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured Interim Construction Financing is considered cash out to the Borrower, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: above $2,000 or 1% of the loan amount, whichever is greater.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Cash-out refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-out refinance Mortgages must meet the requirements in Section 4301.5. Cash￾out refinance Mortgages that are Construction to Permanent Mortgages or Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages must not be secured by Manufactured Homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one Borrower must have been on the title to the land for six months or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the Effective Date of Permanent Financing, or at least one Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have inherited or was legally awarded the land in accordance with a final judgment or
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision from a legal body (e.g., court, jury, judge or arbitrator) such as in a case of
elements.label: text

elements.text: divorce, separation or dissolution of a domestic partnership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must underwrite the Mortgage based on the terms of Permanent Financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If terms of the Permanent Financing are modified or if the appraisal update indicates the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property value has declined, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Loan Product Advisor Mortgage, it may require resubmission of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Loan Product Advisor as described in Section 4602.2(e)(iii), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgage, the Mortgage must be re-underwritten
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Age of documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The age of documentation requirements in Section 5102.4 must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For One-Time Close Construction to Permanent Mortgages, income, employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and credit report documentation may be dated more than 120 days before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Permanent Financing, but dated no more than 540 calendar days
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Effective Date of Permanent Financing, when all of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage must be an Accept Mortgage

elements.text: (g) Appraisal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain an appraisal report with an interior and exterior inspection. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must include an “as completed” value of the property subject to completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the improvements based on the plans and specifications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon completion of the construction or renovations, the Seller must obtain an appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: update reported on Form 442, Appraisal Update and/or Completion Report. Form 442 must
elements.label: text

elements.text: also document that all improvements were completed in accordance with the plans and
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifications and must meet the requirements for verifying completion in Section 5605.8.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The effective date of the appraisal update must be no more than 120 days before the Effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of Permanent Financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the appraisal update indicates the value of the subject property has declined, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must obtain a new appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For One-Time Close Construction to Permanent Mortgages, the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must be no more than 4 months prior to the Note Date of the Interim
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction Financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For One-Time Close Renovation Mortgages, the effective date of the appraisal report must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be no more than 12 months before the Effective Date of Permanent Financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that the originating lender provided the appraiser with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal information required in Topic 5600, including plans and specifications.

elements.text: (a) Overview of One-Time Close transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a One-Time Close transaction, the Note and the Security Instrument documenting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permanent Financing are executed at the time of closing for the Interim Construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing. The terms of the Interim Construction Financing are incorporated into the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the Permanent Financing. A One-Time Close transaction must be converted to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing either through automatic conversion or a modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a One-Time Close transaction, the construction phase of the Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage must be structured as a temporary loan exempt from the ability-to-repay
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under Regulation Z.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) One-Time Close with automatic conversion documentation structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a One-Time Close with automatic conversion, Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically converts to Permanent Financing upon completion of construction or
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations with no change to the terms of the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages must meet the requirements in Section 4301.4,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except as stated below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of Section 4301.4, the amount of the Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text
