elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage as described in Section 4301.4. The proceeds of the Permanent Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be used to pay off a junior lien(s) secured by the Mortgaged Premises provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lien(s) were used in their entirety for the construction or renovation of the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, as applicable, as documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Paying off unsecured lien(s) or construction costs paid by the Borrower outside of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured Interim Construction Financing is considered cash out to the Borrower, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: above $2,000 or 1% of the loan amount, whichever is greater.

elements.text: (B) Cash-out refinance Mortgages

elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the Effective Date of Permanent Financing, or at least one Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have inherited or was legally awarded the land in accordance with a final judgment or
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision from a legal body (e.g., court, jury, judge or arbitrator) such as in a case of
elements.label: text

elements.text: divorce, separation or dissolution of a domestic partnership.

elements.text: (a) Two-Time Close documentation structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a Two-Time Close transaction, the terms of the Interim Construction Financing and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the Permanent Financing are documented on separate sets of loan instruments. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file must include the Note and Security Instrument for the Permanent Financing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must be documented on Uniform Instruments in accordance with Chapter 4101.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be a “no cash-out” or cash-out refinance Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) “No cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages must meet the requirements in Section 4301.4, except
elements.label: text

elements.text: as stated below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of Section 4301.4, the amount of the Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by the Mortgaged Premises is considered an amount used to pay off the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage as described in Section 4301.4. The proceeds of the Permanent Financing may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be used to pay off a junior lien(s) secured by the Mortgaged Premises provided the lien(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: were used in their entirety for the construction or renovation of the subject property, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, as documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Paying off unsecured lien(s) or construction costs paid by the Borrower outside of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured Interim Construction Financing is considered cash out to the Borrower, if above
elements.label: text

elements.text: $2,000 or 1% of the loan amount, whichever is greater.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Cash-out refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-out refinance Mortgages must meet the requirements in Section 4301.5. Cash-out
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgages that are Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages must not be secured by Manufactured Homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one Borrower must have been on the title to the land for six months or more prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Effective Date of Permanent Financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: The six-month minimum titling requirement does not need to be met when
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in the Mortgage file evidences that at least one Borrower inherited or was
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally awarded the land in accordance with a final judgment or decision from a legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: body (e.g., court, jury, judge or arbitrator) such as in a case of divorce, separation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: dissolution of a domestic partnership.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain an appraisal report with an interior and exterior inspection. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must include an “as completed” value of the property subject to completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the improvements based on the plans and specifications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that the originating lender provided the appraiser with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal information required in Topic 5600, including plans and specifications.

elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratios above 95% originated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of the Guide and this chapter.

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Super conforming Mortgages as described in Chapter 4603
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: A HomeOne Mortgage must be either a:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase transaction Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “No cash-out” refinance Mortgage meeting the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage being refinanced must be owned in whole or in part or securitized
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The LTV and/or HTLTV ratio is greater than 95%, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The TLTV ratio is greater than 95% with secondary financing that is not an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Second®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage being refinanced does not have to be owned or securitized by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac when the TLTV ratio is greater than 95% with secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is an Affordable Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: HomeOne Mortgages must be secured by a 1-unit Primary Residence that is not a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home, unless the Manufactured Home is a CHOICEHome®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Occupancy requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne Mortgages, all Borrowers must occupy the Mortgaged Premises as their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne Mortgages, at least one Borrower on the transaction must have a usable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score as determined by Loan Product Advisor®.

elements.text: (g) Maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne Mortgages, the maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratio is 97%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: For HomeOne Mortgages with Affordable Seconds, the maximum TLTV ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: program in accordance with Section 5103.4.

elements.text: HomeOne® Mortgages are Mortgages with loan-to-value (LTV), total LTV (TLTV) and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratios above 95% originated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of the Guide and this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: HomeOne Mortgages eligible for sale to Freddie Mac must be both of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fixed-rate Mortgage; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Accept Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with the following characteristics are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: HomeOne Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refi Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Super conforming Mortgages as described in Chapter 4603
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: A HomeOne Mortgage must be either a:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase transaction Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “No cash-out” refinance Mortgage meeting the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage being refinanced must be owned in whole or in part or securitized
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The LTV and/or HTLTV ratio is greater than 95%, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The TLTV ratio is greater than 95% with secondary financing that is not an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Second®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage being refinanced does not have to be owned or securitized by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac when the TLTV ratio is greater than 95% with secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is an Affordable Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: HomeOne Mortgages must be secured by a 1-unit Primary Residence that is not a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home, unless the Manufactured Home is a CHOICEHome®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Occupancy requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne Mortgages, all Borrowers must occupy the Mortgaged Premises as their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne Mortgages, at least one Borrower on the transaction must have a usable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score as determined by Loan Product Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne Mortgages, the maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratio is 97%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: For HomeOne Mortgages with Affordable Seconds, the maximum TLTV ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne purchase transaction Mortgages, at least one Borrower must be a First￾Time Homebuyer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Homeownership education
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeOne purchase transaction Mortgages when all Borrowers are First-Time
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homebuyers, at least one Borrower must participate in a homeownership education
elements.label: text

elements.text: program in accordance with Section 5103.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.41 for special delivery requirements for HomeOne Mortgages that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase transaction Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.16 for delivery requirements for HomeOne Mortgages that are “no cash￾out” refinance Mortgages.

elements.text: Chapter 4606: GreenCHOICE Mortgages®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4606.1: Eligible Mortgages and improvements, and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: use of Mortgage proceeds (11/06/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that meet the requirements of this chapter and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as GreenCHOICE Mortgages®. This section contains
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of Mortgage proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction Mortgage or a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a property with eligible improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this chapter, “eligible improvements” means:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Energy and/or water efficiency improvements, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Renewable energy sources (e.g., solar panels, hydropower systems, wind turbines and
elements.label: text

elements.text: geothermal systems)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ ENERGY STAR Energy Efficient Products listed at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.energystar.gov/products/products-list
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Electrification improvements (e.g., replacing equipment or appliances that run on
elements.label: text

elements.text: natural gas or other combustible fuel with efficient, all-electric technologies)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Health and safety improvements (e.g., building-code compliance if cited by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable jurisdiction)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resiliency and preventative improvements to either repair natural disaster damage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: improve a home’s ability to withstand future natural disasters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Basic improvements, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Adding ceiling, wall or floor insulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Air conditioning/heating replacement with high efficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Air sealing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Caulking or weather stripping
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Heat pumps and induction cooktops
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ High efficiency refrigerators/freezers, water heaters and light bulbs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Hurricane fabric or shutters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Low-flow water fixtures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Programmable thermostats
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Solar water heaters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Ventilation; radon mitigation; and asbestos, mold or lead abatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Window and door replacement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible improvements must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be permanently affixed to the property, except for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Appliances used in a kitchen, laundry room or utility room
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Health and safety improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not impact the structural integrity of the property for Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes, including Manufactured Homes that are CHOICEHomes®
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Use of Mortgage proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage proceeds must be used as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a purchase transaction Mortgage, to finance the purchase, installation, repair or
elements.label: text

elements.text: upgrade of eligible improvements, to be completed after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage, to finance the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Purchase, installation, repair or upgrade of eligible improvements, to be completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Note Date; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Payment (in whole or in part) towards an existing debt incurred by the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: finance the purchase, installation, repair or upgrade of eligible improvements (such
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt, an “Existing Debt” for purposes of this chapter), completed before the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fees associated with the purchase, installation, repair or upgrade of eligible improvements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including any fees related to plans and specifications, permits, title updates, appraisals, draw
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspections or final inspections, as applicable, may be financed as part of the Mortgage. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: cost of an energy report, if required, may also be financed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be an Accept Mortgage or a Manually Underwritten Mortgage.

elements.text: (c) Qualifying ratios for Manually Underwritten Mortgages
